title: Warmly Spiced Mediterranean Lamb Tagine
description: A one-pot Mediterranean dish featuring lamb, poppy seeds, and a blend of warm spices, cooked in a banana leaf vessel and infused with render fat, making it a kosher-friendly option.
instructions: Heat the rendered lamb fat in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides.
Add the poppy seeds, ground cumin, smoked paprika, dried thyme, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add 1 cup of water to the pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot with a banana leaf, and simmer for 2 hours.
Remove the pot from the heat and let it cool slightly before serving.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
